company.what_they_do: Jean Martin Inc. (JMI) is a global, minority-owned IT services and consulting firm headquartered in New York City (260 Madison Avenue, Manhattan). Founded in 1997, it provides end-to-end technology solutions to enterprise clients across financial services, energy, government, insurance, telecommunications, pharma, manufacturing, and retail. Core service pillars are: (1) Technology Solutions — software engineering, AI/ML, cloud migration, Salesforce, ServiceNow, SAP/Dynamics ERP, CRM, IoT, blockchain, cybersecurity, and mobile/web development; (2) Financial Research & Analysis — investment banking support, PE/VC research, risk, asset management; (3) Data & Analytics — data engineering, data science, BI, visualization; (4) Product Development — Agile dev/ops, QA, testing, documentation. It uses a 'Hybrid Delivery Model' combining on-site and offshore teams. Offshore/global offices span the US (NY, NJ, CA, RI, TX), India (Chennai), Colombia (Bogotá), and Japan. The firm claims 2,000–2,500 employees globally and 500+ clients served.
raw_notes: Founded 1997 by Shawn Kumar (Co-Founder & CEO) and Ravi Kumar (President/Co-Founder).
Minority-owned business; listed with National Minority Business Council.
~150–2,500 employees reported across different sources (varies widely by source/date); Happierleads (July 2026) says 158; LinkedIn says 201–500; JMI's own site says 2,500+.
Revenue estimates vary: D&B says ~$20M, ZoomInfo says ~$79.5M, Happierleads says $10M–$50M.

Key technology specialties per LinkedIn: IT services, CRM, Digital Marketing, Microsoft Dynamics, Mobile app dev, Managed services, IT Infrastructure, ERP, CPQ, AI, IoT, Cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP), Blockchain, Salesforce, Data Analytics, Data Visualization.
JMI Digital arm handles integrated digital roadmaps: SEO, SEM, social, tag management, web analytics.
JMI has built and hosts SaaS solutions (e.g., energy management platform for 25+ utility customers globally).
In March 2024, JMI appointed Kaushik Paul as Chief Revenue Officer (per PR Newswire/Tracxn).
Past partnership with OKTO on security token solutions (2018).
Clients span retail (1,000+ location deployments), manufacturing, utilities, and financial services sectors.
Hybrid model emphasized: minimum on-site presence + strong offshore team for cost savings and quick turnaround.
